name: g15_transport_face_projection
purpose: Record the intrinsic projection law by which the transport object exhibits a stable tetrahedral 3-plus-1 face decomposition across admissible lenses.
core_claim: Faces belong to the projection, not to the camera.

separation_of_layers.quadratic_geometry: sector overlap geometry on G15 via M and Q = MM^T
separation_of_layers.signed_memory: companion signed 2-lift / cocycle on G30 -> G15

projection_law.name: tetra_3_plus_1_projection
projection_law.statement: Under admissible projections, the transport object exhibits three assembling faces and one closure face.

interpretation.F3: closure face folded in to complete tetrahedral closure

invariance_claim.statement: The 3-plus-1 face decomposition is a projection invariant of the transport object and is not created by viewer orientation.

camera_rule.statement: A camera reveals a chosen orientation of an already-defined projected object. It does not assign primitive face roles.

relation_to_existing_specs.transport_emergence_stack: Places face emergence at projection and chirality emergence at transport.
relation_to_existing_specs.transport_face_assignment: Provides tetra-native face labels F0..F3 with F3 as closure face.
relation_to_existing_specs.transport_shell_grammar: Provides shell-region projections of the same transport object.
relation_to_existing_specs.transport_state_machine: Provides the lifted state carrying sheet memory.

chirality_note.statement: This file does not define chirality. Chirality belongs to oriented transport retained by the lift, not to camera and not to raw face naming.

tests_for_renderer: Changing camera must not change which face is designated closure face.
Changing lens must preserve the 3-plus-1 decomposition up to projection distortion.
A face label may move on screen, but its projection role must remain stable.
The closure face may become more or less visually obvious, but it must not be reassigned by viewpoint alone.
short_forms: the camera reveals; the projection defines
